mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, consumer spending capacity collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed +0.5% in 70% of triggered months despite the threshold breach, invalidating the labor weakness channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and real single-family sales signal a synchronized housing sector recovery that lifts construction and durable goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day horizons in backtests spanning 2008-2010, or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain throughput, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when applied to late-cycle or recessionary periods in a rolling validation test.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 215.0 triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 65% of threshold-crossing months without subsequent rate hikes, breaking the inflation-fear transmission.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate revenue expectations for positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative alpha in 60% of observed periods when tested against a risk-free benchmark over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero with p-values above 0.10 across all GDP growth quartiles in a 5-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle phases signals capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks that erode profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when industrial production falls during early-cycle recovery periods, showing regime misclassification.
